constraint: POST /checkout p99 = 1.8s; offloading B and C to an in-proc queue (Option A chosen).
topology_evidence: lib/jobs/worker.ts:* — existing worker pool available for reuse

call_graph: app/api/checkout/route.ts:42 — handleCheckout → serviceA.write — synchronous order write (stays on-path)
app/api/checkout/route.ts:51 — handleCheckout → notify.send — to be replaced by queue.enqueue('notify', event)
app/api/checkout/route.ts:58 — handleCheckout → analytics.record — to be replaced by queue.enqueue('analytics', event)
lib/jobs/worker.ts:* — existing worker pool available for reuse as the consumer side
defaults_invariants: no-net-new-infra — reuse the existing worker pool, do not introduce an external bus
flag-gate-required — cutover ships behind CHECKOUT_ASYNC with documented rollback

sketches.label: A
sketches.title: In-proc queue (consolidated)
sketches.summary: One handler, one queue, two consumers. Idempotency key derived from orderId + topic.
sketches.sequence_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  Client->>+Handler: POST /checkout
  Handler->>+ServiceA: write order
  ServiceA-->>-Handler: ok
  Handler->>Queue: enqueue notify
  Handler->>Queue: enqueue analytics
  Handler-->>-Client: 200
  Queue->>Worker: notify event
  Worker-->>Queue: ack
  Queue->>Worker: analytics event
  Worker-->>Queue: ack

files_touched.reason: Add enqueue call behind CHECKOUT_ASYNC flag
Smoke test: enqueue → notify consumer fires once

migration_steps.description: Add JobQueue interface + in-proc impl; gate behind CHECKOUT_ASYNC=off
Instrument producer call in /checkout route behind flag
Wire notify consumer; assert idempotency key uniqueness
Run smoke test with CHECKOUT_ASYNC=on; validate notify fires exactly once

fixture_dependencies: Seeded order record with known orderId for smoke test
Stub notify-service endpoint (or real service reachable in CI)

verdict.reason: One boundary crossed (handler → queue → notify consumer); all fixtures creatable inline.

consolidated.migration:
1. Add JobQueue interface and in-proc worker pool config (no-op behind flag CHECKOUT_ASYNC=off).
2. Add producer call in /checkout behind the flag; consumers wired but flag-gated.
3. Parallel-run for 48h: flag on for 1% traffic, compare p99 + DLQ rate vs control.
4. Ramp 1% → 10% → 50% → 100% over 5 days if p99 stays under 600ms and DLQ < 0.1%.
5. Cutover: remove sync calls to B and C; keep flag for 14 days for instant rollback.
6. Rollback: flip CHECKOUT_ASYNC=off; sync path re-engages immediately.
slice_tickets: Slice (Turn 3): notify event end-to-end (producer + consumer + smoke test) behind CHECKOUT_ASYNC=on for 1% traffic
Ticket #2: analytics event end-to-end (mirrors notify; ship after notify clears parallel-run)
Ticket #3: cutover PR — remove sync calls once 100% ramp holds for 7 days
parked: Exact retry-backoff constants per topic (current: expo, base 250ms, cap 8s — revisit after load test)
Queue retention window (current default: 24h — confirm against analytics replay needs)
Alert thresholds: DLQ rate, worker lag (set p99 worker lag < 30s)
Dashboard panel layout (one row per topic: enqueue rate, ack rate, DLQ rate, p99 lag)
Correlation id propagation (request_id flows into CheckoutEvent — confirm logging field name)
Worker pool sizing (current: 8 workers × 2 concurrency — re-tune after load test)
DLQ destination details (queue name, retention, alert binding)

question: Ship slice (notify end-to-end), ship skeleton-only, or one tweak?
